howItWorks.intro: This blood alcohol elimination calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: BAC = (Alcohol in grams ÷ (Body Weight kg × r × 10)) - (0.015 × hours drinking × 0.5)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How fast does the body eliminate alcohol?
faq.answer: The average person eliminates alcohol at about 0.015% BAC per hour (roughly one standard drink per hour). This rate is relatively constant and cannot be sped up by coffee, food, or cold showers.

faq.question: What is a standard drink?
faq.answer: One standard drink contains 14 grams of pure alcohol: 12 oz beer (5%), 5 oz wine (12%), or 1.5 oz spirits (40%). Craft beers, large glasses, and strong cocktails may count as 1.5-3 standard drinks.

faq.question: Why do women metabolize alcohol differently?
faq.answer: Women typically have a lower water-to-body-weight ratio (r=0.55 vs 0.68 for men) and less of the enzyme alcohol dehydrogenase. This means the same number of drinks produces a higher BAC in women.

faq.question: Is the Blood Alcohol Elimination Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Blood Alcohol Elimination Calculator?
sections.body: The Blood Alcohol Elimination Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your standard drinks consumed, body weight, gender, you get instant results including estimated peak bac, estimated current bac, time to reach 0.00%. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated peak bac right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your standard drinks consumed and need to find the right estimated peak bac. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Standard Drinks Consumed
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your standard drinks consumed — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: body weight, gender, hours since first drink, drinking duration. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your estimated peak bac, estimated current bac, time to reach 0.00%. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your standard drinks consumed before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
